steps.instruction: 1. Open Fiji 2. Type “set and check” in the search box, select "set and check wrappers," and click "run" 3. Specify correct elastix and transformix paths. These should look something like this (depending on where you installed elastix): C:\elastix-5.0.1-win64\elastix.exe C:\elastix-5.0.1-win64\transformix.exe

steps.title: Download stitched images
steps.instruction: Download stitched images

steps.title: Crop stitched images (2X slide images...
steps.instruction: Crop stitched images (2X slide images only) so that each image only contains one section

steps.title: Create a PowerPoint named with the...
steps.instruction: Create a PowerPoint named with the cohort number and import stitched images, with each PowerPoint slide corresponding to one image.

steps.title: Use crop tool on PowerPoint to crop each stitched image
steps.instruction: Use crop tool on PowerPoint to crop each stitched image.

steps.title: Critical Step
steps.instruction: Critical Step: Save each image to a "Cropped" image folder, naming each image according to its position on the slide from right to left and its conditions (The top right tissue on the first slide would be 1.1.1, corresponding to slide 1, row 1, tissue 1). Make sure to save all images in the .TIF format Example: #Cohort_Genotype_Condition_Stain_1.1.1

steps.title: Order images from rostral to caudal
steps.instruction: Order images from rostral to caudal

steps.title: Duplicate the "Cropped" folder
steps.instruction: Duplicate the "Cropped" folder.

steps.title: Create a new folder called "Ordered"
steps.instruction: Create a new folder called "Ordered"

steps.title: Move images from your duplicate of...
steps.instruction: Move images from your duplicate of the "Cropped" folder to the "Ordered" folder based on their order along the rostral-caudal axis, adding numbers corresponding to their order to the beginning of each file name Example: 05#Cohort_Genotype_Condition_Stain_1.1.1

steps.title: At this point, check that images...
steps.instruction: At this point, check that images are in order and correctly oriented (as seen below)

steps.title: Have another person check the ordered image folder before proceeding
steps.instruction: Have another person check the ordered image folder before proceeding. Make sure that the stitched image powerpoint is also present for comparison.

steps.title: Before beginning to use Qupath/ABBA, ensure...
steps.instruction: Before beginning to use Qupath/ABBA, ensure that image file names are correct – you cannot change them once you put them into QuPath!

steps.title: Generally throughout the workflow, ensure that...
steps.instruction: Generally throughout the workflow, ensure that everything you save is routed to the correct place before you click "Save" – moving things after saving can corrupt your ABBA mapping file. Alternative Workflow Option (if you only want to run the ABBA workflow on images with obvious degradation): After ordering images but before proceeding to the next section ("

steps.title: QuPath Start-Up and Image Import"), proceed to "
steps.instruction: QuPath Start-Up and Image Import"), proceed to "

steps.title: Carrying Out Degradation Analysis" and carry...
steps.instruction: Carrying Out Degradation Analysis" and carry out steps 1 through 7 before returning to this point. Move the images you identify into a separate folder (such as "A.1 Ordered with Degradation") before proceeding with "

steps.title: QuPath Start-Up and Image Import" and...
steps.instruction: QuPath Start-Up and Image Import" and carrying out the rest of the workflow.

steps.title: To keep things organized, create three...
steps.instruction: To keep things organized, create three tiers of folders as described below (following the file system of the lab): Tier

steps.title: Conditions [(A
steps.instruction: Conditions [(A. WT; B. Het), (A. SurWT; B. SurHet), etc…] Tier

steps.title: Three separate subfolders for managing output...
steps.instruction: Three separate subfolders for managing output files (name them as listed below: - A. Ordered - B. QuPath and ABBA Projects - C. For Post-ABBA Analysis

steps.title: Drag the "B
steps.instruction: Drag the "B. QuPath and ABBA Projects" folder into the sidebar (empty/black white space on the left). This establishes it as your QuPath project folder. Click "Yes" when it prompts you to "Create project for empty directory?"

steps.title: Open the "A
steps.instruction: Open the "A. Ordered" folder and select all of your images using "Ctrl + A", making sure to click the first image first so that they stay in order

steps.title: Drag images into the left sidebar...
steps.instruction: Drag images into the left sidebar as previously done with the empty folder.

steps.title: Perform steps 7 through 9 within...
steps.instruction: Perform steps 7 through 9 within the import window before clicking "Import."

steps.title: Under the "Image server" drop-down box, select "Bio-Formats builder
steps.instruction: Under the "Image server" drop-down box, select "Bio-Formats builder."

steps.title: Under the "Set image type" drop-down box, select "Fluorescence"
steps.instruction: Under the "Set image type" drop-down box, select "Fluorescence"

steps.title: Ensure that the Auto-generate pyramids box is not checked
steps.instruction: Ensure that the Auto-generate pyramids box is not checked.

steps.title: Double-click on an image's filename within...
steps.instruction: Double-click on an image's filename within the list on the left. This should make it appear in the image viewer on the right side of the window.

steps.title: Click on the "Image" tab (listed...
steps.instruction: Click on the "Image" tab (listed within the 5 tabs on the top left of the window)

steps.title: Double-click on the number listed for "Pixel width"
steps.instruction: Double-click on the number listed for "Pixel width"

steps.title: Change the pixel width AND pixel...
steps.instruction: Change the pixel width AND pixel height to 5 µm, then press "Ok"

steps.title: Select the Workflow" tab at the...
steps.instruction: Select the Workflow" tab at the top left, then click on the "Create Script" button on the bottom left. The "Script Editor" box will appear, and you should see the commands to set the image type to fluorescence and change the pixel scale for an image.

steps.title: Along the top toolbar, click "Run"...
steps.instruction: Along the top toolbar, click "Run" and then "Run for project"

steps.title: Click the button with the ">>"...
steps.instruction: Click the button with the ">>" symbol in the middle of the pop-up box and then click "Ok" Note: The preceding steps should batch process all images in the same way you processed the first (by setting the image size manually). This will make things more uniform when you enter ABBA.

steps.title: Exit the "Script Editor" box and...
steps.instruction: Exit the "Script Editor" box and return to the "Project" tab

steps.title: Double click on another image's filename...
steps.instruction: Double click on another image's filename (other than the one you double-clicked initially) and select the "Image" tab. Under "Pixel Width" and "Pixel Height", it should say 5 µm.

steps.title: Select "File" - "Save" and close QuPath
steps.instruction: Select "File" - "Save" and close QuPath

steps.title: Align tissues with their corresponding atlas...
steps.instruction: Align tissues with their corresponding atlas slices so that at least one hemisphere looks approximately correct by dragging one or multiple selected tissues left or right. Tissues should already be in order from rostral to caudal, so this process should be relatively straightforward. Video 5.

steps.title: Adjusting R/C Position
steps.instruction: Adjusting R/C Position

steps.title: In positioning mode, find a tissue...
steps.instruction: In positioning mode, find a tissue where the hippocampus is distinctly asymmetrical.

steps.title: Using the "Atlas Slicing" controls in...
steps.instruction: Using the "Atlas Slicing" controls in the bottom right of the display, adjust the X Rotation and Y Rotation so that the hemisphere-to-hemisphere asymmetry of the atlas slices matches the asymmetry of your tissues. Video 5.

steps.title: Adjusting Atlas Slicing
steps.instruction: Adjusting Atlas Slicing

steps.title: Adjust X Rotation for dorsal-ventral asymmetry
steps.instruction: Adjust X Rotation for dorsal-ventral asymmetry

steps.title: Adjust Y Rotation for left-right asymmetry
steps.instruction: Adjust Y Rotation for left-right asymmetry

steps.title: After selecting one or several slices...
steps.instruction: After selecting one or several slices (if they are all "off" by a similar amount from the atlas) in review mode, Click "Align – Interactive Transform" in order to scale and transform tissues to match the atlas slices. This step is the most time-consuming part of registration in ABBA, as you will likely have to make adjustments to images one-by-one, but it is critical in order to achieve a good registration outcome. Video 5.

steps.title: Interactive Transformation
steps.instruction: Interactive Transformation

steps.title: Adjust rotation angle, X scale, Y...
steps.instruction: Adjust rotation angle, X scale, Y scale, X translate, and Y translate as necessary

steps.title: After interactive transformation is complete for...
steps.instruction: After interactive transformation is complete for all images, you should save the current state of the project. Name the saved state as State-1_Interactive-Transformation-Complete". Click OK. Video 5.

steps.title: Saving a Mapping State
steps.instruction: Saving a Mapping State

steps.title: Enable the atlas map (channel
steps.instruction: Enable the atlas map (channel

steps.title: and turn off channels 0 and
steps.instruction: and turn off channels 0 and

steps.title: Select all tissues by clicking the...
steps.instruction: Select all tissues by clicking the first image in the image set table (on the right), and then by pushing "Ctrl + A".

steps.title: Parameters for Affine Registration Atlas channels...
steps.instruction: Parameters for Affine Registration Atlas channels – use channel 2 only. Slices channel – use whatever channel slices are shown in (either 0 or 1). Registration re-sampling (micrometers) – use 15. Show registration results as ImagePlus – this should NOT be checked. Background offset value – use 0. Note: The green circles above each image will turn red, this means the registration is in-process for this image. As they complete, the circles will go from yellow to green.

steps.title: Once Affine Registration is complete, you...
steps.instruction: Once Affine Registration is complete, you should save the current state of the project. Name the saved state as "State-2_Affine-Registration-Complete". Click OK.

steps.title: Registration re-sampling (micrometers) – use
steps.instruction: Registration re-sampling (micrometers) – use

steps.title: Background offset value – use
steps.instruction: Background offset value – use

steps.title: Show registration results as ImagePlus –...
steps.instruction: Show registration results as ImagePlus – this should NOT be checked. Note: The green circles above each image will turn red, this means the registration is in-process for this image. As they complete, the circles will go from yellow to green.

steps.title: Once Spline Registration is complete, you...
steps.instruction: Once Spline Registration is complete, you should save the current project state. Name the saved state as "State-3_Spline-Registration-Complete". Click OK.

steps.title: Preparing File Sets
steps.instruction: Preparing File Sets

steps.title: Open the cohort folder in which...
steps.instruction: Open the cohort folder in which you are working and select the condition you just completed mapping for in ABBA

steps.title: Open the "A
steps.instruction: Open the "A. Ordered" folder, click on the first image, and press "Ctrl + A" followed by "Ctrl + C". Make sure to deselect any BFMEMO files that this step selects and only include the .TIF files. (This can be accomplished by holding the "Shift" key and clicking any files you wish to exclude)

steps.title: Go up one level, open the "C
steps.instruction: Go up one level, open the "C. For Post-ABBA Analysis" folder, and paste the images you just copied into this folder.

steps.title: Slowly click the first image twice; copy the image filename
steps.instruction: Slowly click the first image twice; copy the image filename.

steps.title: Create a new folder (Shift +...
steps.instruction: Create a new folder (Shift + Ctrl + N); paste the name of the first image as the new folder name.

steps.title: Repeat this process for all newly mapped images
steps.instruction: Repeat this process for all newly mapped images.

steps.title: Drag and drop each individual ROIset
steps.instruction: Drag and drop each individual ROIset.zip folder into its respective subfolder within "C. For Post-ABBA Analysis". (Ensure that filenames match)

steps.title: Drag and drop each image (
steps.instruction: Drag and drop each image (.TIF format) into its respective subfolder. (Ensure that filenames match)

steps.title: Open Fiji (if not already open)
steps.instruction: Open Fiji (if not already open)

steps.title: Drag and drop the first image...
steps.instruction: Drag and drop the first image into Fiji to open the image.

steps.title: Slowly click on the ROI set...
steps.instruction: Slowly click on the ROI set zipped folder for the image you just opened; before dragging and dropping into Fiji, add "...RoiSet-Complete" to the name. Example: Original folder name: "062321-TacWT PNN-10X W1-1.4" New folder name: "062321-TacWT_PNN-10X_W1-1.4_RoiSet-Complete"

steps.title: Drag and drop the ROI set...
steps.instruction: Drag and drop the ROI set into Fiji; the ROI manager will open.

steps.title: Click "More" > "Sort" to sort each ROI alphabetically
steps.instruction: Click "More" > "Sort" to sort each ROI alphabetically. This will now sort ROIs by hemisphere Video 9.

steps.title: Delete ROIs which are not pertinent...
steps.instruction: Delete ROIs which are not pertinent to your current work by selecting them and clicking the "Delete" button in the ROI manager. Follow steps 9-14 for details. Video 9.

steps.title: Deleting Irrelevant ROIs
steps.instruction: Deleting Irrelevant ROIs

steps.title: Click on the first ROI listed in the ROI manager
steps.instruction: Click on the first ROI listed in the ROI manager.

steps.title: Scroll down until you find the...
steps.instruction: Scroll down until you find the first ROI which you intend to keep (Primary Motor (MOp) in this case)

steps.title: Hold the "Shift" button on your...
steps.instruction: Hold the "Shift" button on your keyboard, and click the ROI listed right before the ROI you intend to keep (for me, this would be "MO" which shows the full motor cortex subregion); this should highlight all ROIs before MOp.

steps.title: Click the "Delete" button in the...
steps.instruction: Click the "Delete" button in the ROI manager; those ROIs should now be gone.

steps.title: Click on the next ROI which...
steps.instruction: Click on the next ROI which you intend to remove from the manager (below MOp are the ROIs for the layers of this subregion).

steps.title: Scroll until finding the next ROI...
steps.instruction: Scroll until finding the next ROI you intend to keep; shift + click the ROI immediately above it and click on "Delete" in the ROI manager to remove them. Do this repeatedly until you have only the specific ROIs you want to quantify PNN expression in.

steps.title: List of ROIs to keep across...
steps.instruction: List of ROIs to keep across all z-positions (may be updated or changed to any regions in the Allen atlas): SSp-bfd (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Barrel Field Subregion) SSp-ll (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Lower Limb Subregion) SSp-m (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Mouth Subregion) SSp-n (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Nose Subregion) SSp-tr (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Trunk Subregion) SSp-ul (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Upper Limb Subregion) SSp-un (Primary Somatosensory Cortex – Undefined Subregion) SSs (Secondary Somatosensory Cortex) TEa (Temporal Association Subregion)

steps.title: Deselecting ROIs Video 9.2
steps.instruction: Deselecting ROIs Video 9.2: Deselecting ROIs After deleting all non-useful ROIs for your analysis, ensure no ROIs are selected in the manager when the ROI file is saved by performing the following steps: Click on a random ROI in the ROI manager Click the "Deselect" button in the ROI manager

steps.title: Saving ROI Files 1
steps.instruction: Saving ROI Files 1. Ensure you are working in the correct file to save the new ROI set (The folder listed in the "Save in:" dropdown box should match the image name you currently have open in Fiji 2. Add "-Isocortex-YourInitials" as a classifier to the file name; this will be your new ROI set. Example: "RoiSet-Isocortex-LD"

steps.title: Within your open ROI manager that...
steps.instruction: Within your open ROI manager that displays the "RoiSet-Isocortex-##" ROIs, select the first ROI in the list.

steps.title: Click "More" > "Split" Depending on...
steps.instruction: Click "More" > "Split" Depending on the subregion for which you are performing this step, there could be between 2 and 25 new ROIs which appear in the manager. There will be a bunch of small polygons generated (that are located at the edges of ROIs within either hemisphere); we will delete these. The ROI for each subregion will also be generated; we will keep and re-name these.

steps.title: Find the ROIs you intend to...
steps.instruction: Find the ROIs you intend to keep in the ROI manager; rename them to include hemisphere naming nomenclature. An example is shown below using SSp-bfd ROI. Hemisphere-specific ROI nomenclature: "LH SSp-bfd" and "RH SSp-bfd".

steps.title: Delete all other ROIs generated by...
steps.instruction: Delete all other ROIs generated by performing splitting, and delete the hemisphere-combined ROI for the subregion ROIs you just re-named (LH and RH).

steps.title: Repeat this process for all ROIs and all images
steps.instruction: Repeat this process for all ROIs and all images.

steps.title: Click "More" > "Sort" to sort each ROI alphabetically
steps.instruction: Click "More" > "Sort" to sort each ROI alphabetically. This will now sort ROIs by hemisphere

steps.title: Ensure no ROIs are selected in...
steps.instruction: Ensure no ROIs are selected in the manager when the ROI file is saved by performing the following steps: Click on a random ROI in the ROI manager Click the "Deselect" button in the ROI manager Click anywhere within the open image EXCEPT for the ROIs highlighted within the image

steps.title: Click "More" > "Save" and save...
steps.instruction: Click "More" > "Save" and save this ROI set as your final version. Naming nomenclature: "RoiSet-Isocortex-Initials-Final"

steps.title: This file set is now ready for data collection
steps.instruction: This file set is now ready for data collection.

steps.title: Repeat this process for all images
steps.instruction: Repeat this process for all images.

steps.title: Review ordered ChABC images and identify...
steps.instruction: Review ordered ChABC images and identify major areas of PNN depletion A. Sample image from a Penicillinase-injected brain (Control) with no injection-induced degradation. B. Sample image from a ChABC-injected brain with injection-induced degradation within several subregions of the primary somatosensory cortex.

steps.title: Open an image from the "Ordered" folder
steps.instruction: Open an image from the "Ordered" folder

steps.title: Identify regions of the cortex of...
steps.instruction: Identify regions of the cortex of each image where it appears that PNNs are dimmer as compared to the adjacent cortex or other hemisphere

steps.title: Find the biPen image that corresponds...
steps.instruction: Find the biPen image that corresponds to the opened ChABC image

steps.title: Compare the intensity of dim regions...
steps.instruction: Compare the intensity of dim regions of PNNs of the ChABC image with the intensity of PNNs in the corresponding region of the biPen image

steps.title: If the PNNs are much dimmer...
steps.instruction: If the PNNs are much dimmer for the ChABC image than for the biPen image, note the image’s order number

steps.title: Repeat this process for all ChABC...
steps.instruction: Repeat this process for all ChABC images until you have identified all images with depleted PNNs

steps.title: Set-up spreadsheet for analysis (Ours is linked here
steps.instruction: Set-up spreadsheet for analysis (Ours is linked here: Degradation Analysis Spreadsheet.xlsx )

steps.title: Rename the file according to the...
steps.instruction: Rename the file according to the cohort number you are working on

steps.title: At the bottom of the screen,...
steps.instruction: At the bottom of the screen, rename the sheet named "Cohort #" with the cohort number you are working on

steps.title: Fill out the information in cells A4 through D4
steps.instruction: Fill out the information in cells A4 through D4

steps.title: Enter the cohort information in cells E4 through I4
steps.instruction: Enter the cohort information in cells E4 through I4

steps.title: Create a new row of the...
steps.instruction: Create a new row of the spreadsheet for each hemisphere (left and right) of each image in which you have identified PNN depletion, filling out all information from columns E through I Change column E information according to order number Change column H information according to section number (number used to orient tissue on slide (such as 1.1.1 being the first tissue on the first row of the first slide imaged)) Ensure that a row exists for both the left and right hemisphere of each tissue, even if only one hemisphere has depleted PNNs Review the example spreadsheet page to ensure that the spreadsheet is set-up correctly

steps.title: Original ABBA documentation can be found at https://abba-documentation
steps.instruction: Original ABBA documentation can be found at https://abba-documentation.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ A video tutorial of ABBA can be found at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sERGONVw4zE Another protocol involving ABBA and cell density quantification can be found at https://www.protocols.io/view/a-free-analysis-pipeline-to-coregister-3d-lightshe-8epv5rz1ng1b/v1?step=2 Videos of selected steps (also linked individually throughout the protocol): https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLQCli8HoaKipPETVbl_tELMhf9EkPrkEm&si=AhJmpD2i15LEYHIN

steps.title: Slides with installation instructions
steps.instruction: Slides with installation instructions: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1S6U1-yMAoaHALiIuvs90rlktF-OCec8T1xMkt7ej378/edit#slide=id.g10b397560b2_0_175

steps.title: Open Fiji/ImageJ
steps.instruction: Open Fiji/ImageJ

steps.title: In the search bar, type "abba"...
steps.instruction: In the search bar, type "abba" and select "ABBA - ABBA Start" and "Start"

steps.title: Select the appropriate atlas (we used Allen Brain Atlas V3p
steps.instruction: Select the appropriate atlas (we used Allen Brain Atlas V3p

steps.title: and click "Ok"
steps.instruction: and click "Ok"

steps.title: Select the appropriate slicing mode (we...
steps.instruction: Select the appropriate slicing mode (we used coronal) and click "Ok"

steps.title: To move around and select objects within ABBA
steps.instruction: To move around and select objects within ABBA: Left-Click: drag and select Mouse Wheel: zoom Right-Click: pan (hold and drag) Up and Down Keys: zoom in and out

steps.title: The yellow sections you're seeing are the atlas
steps.instruction: The yellow sections you're seeing are the atlas. The atlas has 3 channels: Channel 0 - Nissl staining Channel 1 - Allen Reference Atlas (ARA) - Auto Fluorescence Channel 2 - Borders of regions You can turn these on and off, or increase or decrease the intensity of the layer by using the sliders and boxes on the right control panel

steps.title: Control the displayed spacing between slices...
steps.instruction: Control the displayed spacing between slices with the displayed slicing [microns] slider: 10 steps = 100 microns between slices 50 steps = 500 microns between slices

steps.title: There are two view modes in ABBA
steps.instruction: There are two view modes in ABBA: Positioning Mode (all slices visible): For initial positioning/reordering/rotating of slices along the atlas axis. Review Mode (one slice visible): For review of slice position/in plane registration

steps.title: In the ABBA window menu bar...
steps.instruction: In the ABBA window menu bar select "Import" > "Import QuPath Project" Video 4.

steps.title: Select the appropriate "project
steps.instruction: Select the appropriate "project.qpproj" file from your "B. Qupath and ABBA Projects" folder

steps.title: Specify an initial AP position (This...
steps.instruction: Specify an initial AP position (This is how far into the brain you want the initial position to be; 4 usually works pretty well)

steps.title: Specify a spacing increment between slices...
steps.instruction: Specify a spacing increment between slices (This is how far apart each slice is, which should roughly correlate with thickness – for example, 80 µm sections – 0.08 mm spacing)

steps.title: In the "Create BDV Dataset" pop-up box, just click "Ok"
steps.instruction: In the "Create BDV Dataset" pop-up box, just click "Ok". No changes are needed here.

steps.title: Select all slices using either "shift...
steps.instruction: Select all slices using either "shift + click" or drawing a rectangle around all of the circles and squares in the viewing window

steps.title: Click the "Vis
steps.instruction: Click the "Vis." and "Ch_0" heading boxes on the table (lower right portion of the ABBA window). This should reveal all slices

steps.title: Automated Slice Alignment Workflow Example (Optional)...
steps.instruction: Automated Slice Alignment Workflow Example (Optional) Affine registration on DAPI vs Atlas Nissl (Ch 0) Spline registration on an Autofluorescent channel vs Atlas Autofluorescent (Ch 1) (15 control points) Spline registration on DAPI vs Atlas Nissl (Ch 0) (15 control points) This takes about 10 minutes for 50 slices on a laptop.

steps.title: In the ABBA window, select all slices
steps.instruction: In the ABBA window, select all slices.

steps.title: Select "Export" > "Export Regions to...
steps.instruction: Select "Export" > "Export Regions to File" > Select the appropriate "C. For Post-ABBA Analysis" folder as the location to save all ROI file sets Video 7.

steps.title: Exporting ROI Files
steps.instruction: Exporting ROI Files

steps.title: At this point, it is safe...
steps.instruction: At this point, it is safe to close both ABBA and QuPath. Leave Fiji open if continuing.

steps.title: Open Fiji and drag the first...
steps.instruction: Open Fiji and drag the first image with depleted PNNs into the Fiji interface

steps.title: Remove the scale associated with the...
steps.instruction: Remove the scale associated with the image on Fiji – This step needs to completed each time Fiji is opened Video 10.

steps.title: Select "Analyze" – "Set Scale" – "Click to Remove Scale"
steps.instruction: Select "Analyze" – "Set Scale" – "Click to Remove Scale"

steps.title: Check the box labeled "Global"
steps.instruction: Check the box labeled "Global"

steps.title: Click "Ok"
steps.instruction: Click "Ok"

steps.title: When opening the next image, uncheck...
steps.instruction: When opening the next image, uncheck "Disable Global Calibration" and check "Disable these Messages"

steps.title: Drag the "RoiSet-Isocortex-Initials-Final" file corresponding to...
steps.instruction: Drag the "RoiSet-Isocortex-Initials-Final" file corresponding to the image into the Fiji interface This file is found in the folder corresponding to the image you are analyzing in the "C. For Post-ABBA Analysis" folder

steps.title: Select "Show All" to display the...
steps.instruction: Select "Show All" to display the outlines of each cortical ROI

steps.title: Use the polygon selection tool to...
steps.instruction: Use the polygon selection tool to draw the outline of the portion of an ROI where PNNs are dimmer (as compared to the adjacent cortex, cortex of the other hemisphere, or corresponding biPen image) by clicking to add points and dragging to draw line segments Video 10.

steps.title: Outlining Degradation Begin at the outer...
steps.instruction: Outlining Degradation Begin at the outer edge of the tissue and trace along the path of the ChABC injection to the end of the dim region or ROI (whichever comes first) If a region of dimness intersects with the ROI outline, trace the ROI outline as closely as possible After "drawing" the outline, click the first point you created to complete the polygon Press "T" to save the outlined region that you have drawn In the ROI Manager window, rename the newly saved ROI (which is probably a series of numbers at this point) using the format "(L/R)H-ROI_reduced" Example: left hemisphere reduced SSp-bfd would be called "LH SSp- bfd_reduced" If the region of PNN dimness encompasses multiple ROIs, repeat the drawing and saving process for each affected ROI

steps.title: Save the ROI file within its...
steps.instruction: Save the ROI file within its corresponding folder in the "C. For Post-ABBA Analysis" folder using the format "Roiset-Isocortex-Initials-Final-Analyzed" If you forget to do this, you will have to redraw the reduced areas for each ROI before moving forward!

steps.title: Measure the total area of the...
steps.instruction: Measure the total area of the ROI where PNNs were found to be depleted

steps.title: Select the ROI in the ROI manager window Example
steps.instruction: Select the ROI in the ROI manager window Example: For an image where the left-sided barrel field was depleted, select "LH_SSp-bfd"

steps.title: Select "Measure" along the right side...
steps.instruction: Select "Measure" along the right side of the ROI manager window

steps.title: Record this value in the "Full...
steps.instruction: Record this value in the "Full Area" spreadsheet cell corresponding to the correct tissue, hemisphere, and ROI

steps.title: If the region of PNN dimness...
steps.instruction: If the region of PNN dimness encompasses multiple ROIs, repeat the measurement for each affected ROI

steps.title: Determine the rostral-caudal axis value for each depleted ROI
steps.instruction: Determine the rostral-caudal axis value for each depleted ROI

steps.title: Load the final saved state of...
steps.instruction: Load the final saved state of ABBA mapping for the ChABC animal you are analyzing (found within the "B. QuPath and ABBA Projects" folder for the cohort you are working on) Use the .ABBA file type (no other file type will load)

steps.title: Enter review mode and navigate to...
steps.instruction: Enter review mode and navigate to the tissue that you are currently working on

steps.title: Select "Display" > "Show mouse atlas...
steps.instruction: Select "Display" > "Show mouse atlas position" from the menus along the top of the ABBA window

steps.title: Hover your mouse over the center...
steps.instruction: Hover your mouse over the center of the ROI you are currently analyzing, making sure that you are hovering over the correct hemisphere

steps.title: Record the first of the three...
steps.instruction: Record the first of the three coordinates that appear when you hover over the tissue in the appropriate cell in the spreadsheet column labeled "(ROI) Map" that corresponds to the correct tissue, hemisphere and ROI. Note: This value represents the position of the tissue along the rostral-caudal axis and changes at different points of the same tissue due to asymmetry in sectioning (which are accounted for by adjustments to the "Atlas Slicing Angle," so it is important that this value is recorded from the center of each ROI to remain consistent)

steps.title: If the region of PNN dimness...
steps.instruction: If the region of PNN dimness encompasses multiple ROIs, repeat the rostral-caudal axis determination for each affected ROI

steps.title: Measure the area of the reduced...
steps.instruction: Measure the area of the reduced ROI that you just outlined and saved

steps.title: Select the "(L/R)H_ROI _ reduced" ROI in the ROI manager
steps.instruction: Select the "(L/R)H_ROI _ reduced" ROI in the ROI manager

steps.title: Select "Measure" along the right side...
steps.instruction: Select "Measure" along the right side of the ROI manager window

steps.title: Record this value in the "Depleted...
steps.instruction: Record this value in the "Depleted Area" spreadsheet cell corresponding to the correct tissue, hemisphere, and ROI

steps.title: If the region of PNN dimness...
steps.instruction: If the region of PNN dimness encompasses multiple ROIs, repeat steps 1-26 for each affected ROI

steps.title: Repeat steps 1 through 27 for...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 1 through 27 for each image with PNN depletion NOTE: If you are analyzing images that fall between images with PNN depletion but do not have any depletion themselves, include a reduced area of 0 in the corresponding "Depleted Area" spreadsheet cell for that image

steps.title: Locate the cell in the "%...
steps.instruction: Locate the cell in the "% Depleted" part of the spreadsheet that corresponds to the tissue, hemisphere, and ROI that you have been analyzing

steps.title: Create a formula that divides the...
steps.instruction: Create a formula that divides the depleted area of the ROI you calculated and recorded in step 12 by the total area of the ROI that you calculated and recorded in step 10 (to represent the proportion of the total ROI where PNNs were dimmer than "normal")

steps.title: Use the format "=(Z16/J16)"
steps.instruction: Use the format "=(Z16/J16)"

steps.title: In the "Example" page of the...
steps.instruction: In the "Example" page of the analysis spreadsheet, this represents the proportion of an SSp-bfd ROI that was depleted

steps.title: Ensure that the formula includes the...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the formula includes the correct total and reduced area cells

steps.title: In the first cell of an...
steps.instruction: In the first cell of an ROI’s "% Depleted" column, click and drag down from the corner of the first cell with the manually entered formula to apply the formula to the remaining cells in the column

steps.title: Repeat as necessary to ensure that...
steps.instruction: Repeat as necessary to ensure that the reduction proportion is calculated for all ROIs with PNN depletion

steps.title: Select all cells within the "%...
steps.instruction: Select all cells within the "% Depleted" area of the spreadsheet then change the number format to "Percentage" within the menu options of Excel to convert the calculated number from a proportion to a percentage

steps.title: If the region of PNN dimness...
steps.instruction: If the region of PNN dimness encompasses multiple ROIs, repeat as needed for each affected ROI

steps.title: Make sure that the ChABC analysis...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the ChABC analysis spreadsheet and all files from the ABBA process are saved to the server and checked as needed
